items.title: IOS-XE Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device reachability and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Successful ping to the device and/or gateway; interface shows line protocol up.

steps.action: Verify interface status and configuration.
steps.expected: Interface is administratively up/line protocol up with correct IP assignment.

steps.action: Check routing table and next-hop reachability.
steps.expected: Correct route present with active next-hop and outgoing interface.

steps.action: Validate static route if configured.
steps.expected: Static route appears in routing table with correct next-hop or exit interface.

steps.action: Inspect control-plane and management plane health.
steps.expected: CPU/memory within normal ranges; no critical alarms.

steps.action: Verify SSH/HTTPS management access and users.
steps.expected: SSH/HTTPS sessions established; authorized users present.

steps.action: Confirm system time and NTP sync.
steps.expected: Clock is synchronized; stratum reasonable.

steps.action: Check platform-specific features (FlexVPN/GETVPN/IoT).
steps.expected: Feature adjacencies formed; SA/Policy established as expected.

steps.action: Review diagnostics and health for hardware/software issues.
steps.expected: No critical hardware faults; software version matches expected image.

steps.action: Apply minimal config change and verify impact.
steps.expected: Change applied without disruption; desired reachability/behavior restored.

steps.action: Verify Nexus service status and basic connectivity.
steps.expected: Nexus is running and reachable on expected ports.

steps.action: Check Nexus logs for errors or warnings.
steps.expected: No critical errors; logs show normal startup.

steps.action: Validate repository configuration and connectivity to remote repositories.
steps.expected: Repositories are correctly configured and reachable.

steps.action: Inspect blob store health and storage usage.
steps.expected: Blob store is healthy with adequate free space.

steps.action: Review and adjust JVM and Nexus heap settings if needed.
steps.expected: Heap settings are within recommended limits for available RAM.

steps.action: Test artifact deployment and retrieval end-to-end.
steps.expected: Artifact can be deployed and downloaded successfully.

steps.action: Verify security and access policies (roles, privileges, tokens).
steps.expected: Roles and privileges align with intended access controls.

steps.action: Check scheduled tasks and maintenance settings.
steps.expected: Tasks such as blob store cleanup and repair are scheduled appropriately.

steps.action: Validate network/firewall and proxy settings if external access fails.
steps.expected: Outbound connections succeed; proxy settings are correct.

steps.action: Rotate and archive logs if needed, then restart Nexus to apply changes.
steps.expected: Logs rotated, configuration changes active, Nexus stable.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and power
steps.expected: WLC is powered on, LEDs indicate normal operation, and Ethernet link LEDs are active.

steps.action: Access the WLC CLI via console or SSH
steps.expected: Successful login to the WLC command-line interface.

steps.action: Check system status and logs
steps.expected: No critical alarms; system uptime and resources are normal.

steps.action: Verify interface configurations
steps.expected: Management interface has correct IP, gateway, and VLAN settings.

steps.action: Validate AP connectivity and join status
steps.expected: APs are in 'RUN' state and associated with the WLC.

steps.action: Check RF and client configurations
steps.expected: Radio profiles, WLANs, and client settings are correctly configured.

steps.action: Test client association and connectivity
steps.expected: Client can associate to WLAN and obtain IP via DHCP with full connectivity.

steps.action: Review and apply firmware/software updates if needed
steps.expected: WLC runs recommended firmware version with no known bugs.

steps.action: Save configuration and document changes
steps.expected: Running config saved to startup-config and change log updated.

items.title: PAN-OS Basic Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device connectivity and management access.
steps.expected: Management interface is reachable and you can log in via SSH/HTTPS.

steps.action: Verify PAN-OS software version and license status.
steps.expected: Device runs a supported PAN-OS version and license is valid.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Interfaces are administratively up/up with correct IP/subnet.

steps.action: Validate routing and default gateway.
steps.expected: Routing table contains correct next-hop/default route.

steps.action: Test reachability to external resources.
steps.expected: Ping/traceroute to external IP/DNS succeeds.

steps.action: Check security policies and allow traffic.
steps.expected: Policy rules permit intended traffic between zones.

steps.action: Enable logging and verify traffic logs.
steps.expected: Logs show allowed/blocked sessions as expected.
commands: set logging setting filter rule <rule-name> any any any any any no-log disable

steps.action: Check HA status if High Availability is configured.
steps.expected: HA state is active/passive or active/active as configured.

steps.action: Commit configuration and verify deployment.
steps.expected: Commit succeeds and changes are active on device(s).

steps.action: Save configuration to ensure persistence across reboot.
steps.expected: Configuration is saved and will survive reboot.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and power.
steps.expected: Device is powered on, all LEDs indicate normal operation (no fault LEDs). Management interface has link.

steps.action: Check interface status and IP configuration.
steps.expected: Management interface has correct IP, netmask, gateway, and is administratively up.

steps.action: Test reachability of the device.
steps.expected: Ping to management IP from a host succeeds.

steps.action: Confirm Panorama connectivity (if applicable).
steps.expected: Device shows connected to Panorama with accepted template stack.

steps.action: Check software version and availability of updates.
steps.expected: Running a supported, non-EOL PAN-OS version with latest updates applied.

steps.action: Review system logs for errors.
steps.expected: No critical or repeated errors; traffic and threat logs reflect expected activity.

steps.action: Validate virtual router and routing table.
steps.expected: Default route or expected next-hop present; virtual router shows correct interface zones.

steps.action: Check security policies and rule hits.
steps.expected: Policies reflect intended allow/deny logic; hit counts increase with matching traffic.

steps.action: Verify NAT rules and address objects.
steps.expected: Source/destination NAT rules translate addresses as designed; no overlapping IPs.

steps.action: Confirm decryption and SSL/TLS settings.
steps.expected: Decryption policies, SSL Forward Proxy, or SSL Inbound Inspection are correctly configured with valid certificates.

steps.action: Check HA status (if HA is enabled).
steps.expected: Active/Passive or Active/Active state is stable with sync in progress or completed.

steps.action: Validate threat prevention and URL filtering.
steps.expected: Threat prevention profiles and URL filtering are applied to policies; signatures and categories are up to date.

steps.action: Run diagnostics and capture traffic if needed.
steps.expected: Diagnostics complete; packet captures show expected traffic flows and issues.

steps.action: Commit changes and monitor post-commit stability.
steps.expected: Commit succeeds without errors; device remains responsive and policies take effect.

items.title: IOS-XE Basic Connectivity Troubleshooting and Configuration Guide

steps.action: Check device status and interfaces
steps.expected: Verify device is powered, fans and LEDs are normal; interfaces show appropriate link status.

steps.action: Verify physical connectivity and cable integrity
steps.expected: Link/up on both ends for the relevant interface.

steps.action: Check Layer 3 configuration and reachability
steps.expected: Correct IP addressing, subnet, and default gateway; reachable next-hop.

steps.action: Validate ARP and MAC learning
steps.expected: ARP entry present; MAC learned on the correct interface.

steps.action: Confirm DHCP or static IP assignment
steps.expected: Host receives correct IP configuration via DHCP or static config.

steps.action: Check for access-lists / firewall filtering
steps.expected: No deny ACEs blocking required traffic; inspection policies as intended.

steps.action: Verify routing protocol state (if used)
steps.expected: Neighbors established; routes installed.

steps.action: Review logging and diagnostics
steps.expected: No critical/emergency errors; relevant informational messages present.

steps.action: Apply minimal config to restore connectivity (if needed)
steps.expected: Interface is administratively up/up with correct IP; traffic flows.
